title: Improve volume selection for podman volume prune
body:
### Feature request description

Hi,

when working with podman, podman creates a heap of "anomymous" volumes with random hex-string names. podman volume inspect show something like


podman volume inspect 23dbf5a46d4cc628d53c4780ef6737bfc9dcb8254937890a5f304d66096de34e
[
     {
          "Name": "23dbf5a46d4cc628d53c4780ef6737bfc9dcb8254937890a5f304d66096de34e",
          "Driver": "local",
          "Mountpoint": "/home/hadmut/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/23dbf5a46d4cc628d53c4780ef6737bfc9dcb8254937890a5f304d66096de34e/_data",
          "CreatedAt": "2026-05-22T00:09:51.145141641+02:00",
          "Labels": {},
          "Scope": "local",
          "Options": {},
          "Anonymous": true,
          "MountCount": 0,
          "NeedsCopyUp": true,
          "NeedsChown": true,
          "LockNumber": 39
     }
]


but they don't have a label or option which can be selected by the prune command's filters. 

On the other hand, "Anonymous": true clearly identifies those volumes, but podman volume prune has no filter to select just them. 


It would, furthermore, be really important to have a dry-run option (as so many programs with possibly destructive effects do offer), to just list what would have been done, without actually doing it. For checking filter definitions. 


The main problem is, that podman creates a big heap of trash volumes, making deleting them manually annoying, while non-anomyous volumes might contain important data and could erroneously be deleted. 





### Suggest potential solution

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: We've changed the default behavior of `podman volume prune` in Podman 6. [By default, only anonymous (unnamed) **unused** volumes are removed](https://docs.podman.io/en/v6.0.1/markdown/podman-volume-prune.1.html#:~:text=By%20default%20only%20anonymous%20(unnamed)%20unused%20volumes%20are%20removed).
